title: iPhone Fold launching soon: Expected price, display, camera, colours and other specifications
snippet: The iPhone Fold is expected to launch in September alongside the iPhone 18 Pro, with a price range of $2,000 to $2,500. It features a foldable design, A20 Pro chipset, and quad camera setup, while lacking FaceID and supporting only eSIM.

title: OnePlus Nord 6 India launch tomorrow:Expected price, specifications and live-stream details
snippet: OnePlus Nord 6 will be debuting in India at 7PM tomorrow. Ahead of the launch, take a look at the expected price, specifications and other details about the device.

title: ‘Argue with Dario’: Anthropic's head of growth says Claude maker encourages employees to call out its CEO publicly
snippet: Anthropic's Head of Growth Amol Avasarala has revealed that the AI startup encourages employees to openly disagree with leadership. He noted that this culture of transparency through Slack channels fosters debate and trust, contributing to the company's rapid growth and success

title: iPhone 17 now available for less than  ₹50,000 during latest Croma sale: How to get the deal?
snippet: Croma is offering significant discounts on the iPhone 17, bringing its effective price to  ₹44,768. The device come with &nbsp;a 6.3-inch 120Hz OLED display, dual 48MP camera setup and is powered by the A19 chipset with Apple Intelligence support.&nbsp;

title: Infinix Note 60 Pro with ‘Active Matrix Display’ confirmed to launch in India: Pre-book benefits, specs and features
snippet: Infinix will launch its mid-range Note 60 Pro in India on April 13. The company is offering pre-booking offers include up to  ₹7,000 in benefits for the phone including a MagSafe speaker and exclusive bank discount.

title: Are your apps secretly stealing your data? FBI warns iPhone and Android users over Chinese mobile apps
snippet: The FBI has warned of risks linked to downloading mobile apps, particularly those developed in China. The agency says such apps could expose user data to the Chinese government, raising privacy concerns.

title: Mint Explainer | India finds a space surveillance market. Why regulations may pose a challenge

title: Apple 50th anniversary sale: iPhone 17 drops below  ₹50,000, iPhone 17 Pro Max available at  ₹1,02,900
snippet: Apple celebrates 50 years with discounts on iPhones including the iPhone 17 and Pro Max. Customers can benefit from trade-in bonuses and cashback offers

title: Claude AI can now search your Outlook emails and Teams chats for free: Here's how it works
snippet: Anthropic has enabled all Claude users to access the Microsoft 365 connector. The feature was previously available only to the company's Team and Enterprise users. With the new integration users can search and analyze data from Microsoft apps without manual uploading the files.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta builds new AI hardware divison, hires hires veteran engineer for mysterious AI gadget: Report
snippet: Meta is assembling a hardware team under its Superintelligence Labs and is hiring Rui Xu from Dreamer to lead the effort. The move is said to indicate the company's interest in developing AI gadgets beyond beyond smart glasses and VR headsets.

title: Apple's foldable iPhone faces engineering snags, potential shipment delays: Nikkei Asia
snippet: Apple has been facing technical challenges while testing its first foldable iPhone, which may delay production and release. Early engineering issues mean more time is needed for fixes. The foldable model was expected around late 2026, alongside other upgraded iPhones, but timelines could slip.

title: AI-generated artists break through in country music
snippet: AI-generated country singers are gaining popularity by mimicking modern, formulaic sounds. These songs are easy to produce and often lack deep storytelling, worrying human songwriters. While some listeners don’t mind, others value authenticity. Experts believe emotional, traditional styles may help

title: Australia's Firmus raises $505 million to expand AI infrastructure across Asia-Pacific
snippet: The company plans to use the funds to ​scale its AI infrastructure platform across the ​Asia-Pacific region. title: SpaceX lays out IPO details, targets early June roadshow
snippet: SpaceX outlined details of its highly anticipated IPO at a meeting with its team of bankers Monday night, telling them it plans to earmark a large portion of shares for retail investors and will host 1,500 of them at an event in June following the IPO ‌roadshow launch.

title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
snippet: Nvidia last December announced it would acquire SchedMD, giving it control ​of open-source software called Slurm, which schedules computing tasks and is critical for training large language models that power chatbots such as Anthropic's Claude. title: Anthropic tops $30 billion run rate, seals deal with Broadcom
snippet: Anthropic PBC said its revenue run rate has now topped $30 billion, up from $9 billion at the end of 2025, and confirmed plans to work with Broadcom and Google to power its burgeoning operations.

title: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google unite to combat model copying in China
snippet: Rivals OpenAI, Anthropic PBC, and Alphabet’s Google have begun working together to try to clamp down on Chinese competitors extracting results from cutting-edge US artificial intelligence models to gain an edge in the global AI race. title: Netflix debuts new Playground gaming app for kids
snippet: Netflix doubled down on its gaming ​efforts on Monday, launching ​a new app called "Netflix Playground" that would feature games ​built around popular children's characters such as Peppa Pig and Sesame Street.

title: India's tech hiring chills; Polymarket's IPL bet

title: AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.
